mood.label: CAUTIOUSLY-RISK-ON
mood.summary: Breadth is positive and semiconductors are leading, but the Fed decision/press conference is the dominant tape risk. Treat new entries as conditional triggers only; do not chase extended high-RVOL names after the opening move.

indicesNarrative: SPY/QQQ/IWM are green-to-flat with fair ATR entry risk, while DIA is already extended at +2.09 ATR from the 21EMA. Breadth is supportive, but the 14:00 ET FOMC decision and 14:30 ET Warsh press conference argue for waiting for post-event confirmation.
technicalsNarrative: SPY, QQQ, IWM, and SMH are all FAIR by the ATR/21EMA model; DIA is the worst chase risk at EXTENDED. IWM has a bear-cross-imminent flag despite a fair distance reading, so small-cap longs still need tight triggers rather than market buys.

sectorsNarrative: Leadership is concentrated in semiconductors/equipment and selected industrials/financials. Communication services, energy, staples, and healthcare ETFs are lagging, so the tape is constructive but not one-way risk-on.
industryNarrative: Electronic Technology and Producer Manufacturing have the strongest breadth/read-through via SMH, AMAT, LRCX, ASML and JBL. Energy Minerals and Communications are the weakest breadth groups, so avoid broad sympathy longs there.

standout.thesis: Only production screener GO: EP pattern, +11.8% intraday, RVOL 41.7x, and score 83. Because same-day catalyst is not cleanly verified, entry is conditional above the intraday high/ORH rather than a market buy.
